No Poverty.reason: The course equips students with research methods to investigate social phenomena and processes, including poverty, supported by a core competency in humanistic and social concern, which can inform pro-poor policies.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: Sociological research methods taught in this course are applicable to investigating social phenomena related to health disparities, access to care, and well-being, supporting health risk management.

Quality Education.reason: As a graduate-level course, it directly provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and equips students with foundational research skills for academic and professional careers (Target 4.4).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course enhances scientific research capacity by teaching empirical research methods, directly contributing to increasing the number of research workers and fostering innovation (Target 9.5).

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course explicitly covers 'Research Ethics and IRB,' promoting accountable practices (Target 16.6), and provides methods to analyze institutions and access to justice (Target 16.3).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course directly builds capacity for designing rigorous studies and collecting high-quality data, a core component of Target 17.18 on increasing reliable data for development.
